name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Management Protocol
description: An evidence-based clinical protocol for the comprehensive management of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM) in primary care, adhering to the ADA 2024 Standards of Care in Diabetes.

templateData.duration: Initial phase: 3-6 months for glycemic stabilization and lifestyle modification. Ongoing: Lifelong management.
goals: Achieve and maintain individualized HbA1c target (e.g., <7.0% for most adults, <6.5% for select individuals, <8.0% for those with comorbidities/hypoglycemia risk).
Achieve and maintain blood pressure <130/80 mmHg (for most adults with T2DM and hypertension).
Achieve and maintain LDL-C <1.8 mmol/L (<70 mg/dL) for patients 40-75 years with ASCVD or multiple risk factors.
Achieve and maintain body weight reduction of ≥5% for overweight/obese individuals.
Prevent or delay the progression of diabetes-related complications (e.g., nephropathy, retinopathy, neuropathy).
Minimize hypoglycemia risk and improve quality of life.

items: Referral to Medical Nutrition Therapy (MNT) with a Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN).
Structured physical activity program (e.g., 150 minutes/week of moderate-intensity aerobic activity, 2-3 sessions/week of resistance training).
Stress management techniques and mental health screening.
Regular self-monitoring of blood glucose (SMBG) or continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) as indicated.
Consider GLP-1 Receptor Agonist (GLP-1 RA) or SGLT2 Inhibitor (SGLT2i) early, especially in patients with established ASCVD, heart failure, or chronic kidney disease.
Individualize medication selection based on comorbidities, glucose-lowering efficacy, hypoglycemia risk, impact on body weight, and cost.
Initiate ACE inhibitor or ARB for hypertension with albuminuria.
Statin therapy for dyslipidemia (moderate-intensity for adults 40-75 years without ASCVD, high-intensity for those with ASCVD).
Aspirin therapy (75-162 mg/day) for secondary prevention in patients with established ASCVD; consider for primary prevention in select high-risk patients.
Annual foot examination (monofilament test, vibratory sensation, pulses).
Annual urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) and estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR).
Referral to diabetes self-management education and support (DSMES) program.

medications.name: Metformin
medications.dosage: 500 mg once or twice daily, titrate up to 2000 mg daily (max 2550 mg daily) as tolerated.
medications.instructions: Start low and titrate slowly to minimize gastrointestinal side effects. Discontinue if eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m². Hold prior to iodinated contrast procedures for patients with eGFR 30-60 mL/min/1.73m².

medications.name: Semaglutide (GLP-1 RA)
medications.dosage: 0.25 mg once weekly, titrate to 0.5 mg, then 1 mg, then 2 mg once weekly as tolerated.
medications.instructions: Administer on the same day each week, any time of day, with or without meals. May cause nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea. Contraindicated in patients with a personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) or Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia syndrome type 2 (MEN 2).

medications.name: Empagliflozin (SGLT2i)
medications.dosage: 10 mg once daily, may increase to 25 mg once daily.
medications.instructions: Take in the morning with or without food. Ensure adequate hydration. Monitor for genitourinary infections, euglycemic DKA, and volume depletion. Not recommended for eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m² for glycemic control.

medications.name: Lisinopril (ACE Inhibitor)
medications.dosage: 10 mg once daily, titrate to target blood pressure.
medications.instructions: Monitor blood pressure, renal function, and serum potassium closely. May cause cough or angioedema. Contraindicated in pregnancy.

lifestyle: Adopt a balanced eating pattern (e.g., Mediterranean, DASH, plant-based) focusing on whole foods, lean protein, and healthy fats.
Limit intake of refined carbohydrates, added sugars, and saturated/trans fats.
Engage in at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity (e.g., brisk walking, swimming).
Incorporate 2-3 sessions per week of resistance training (e.g., lifting weights, bodyweight exercises).
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight; aim for a 5-10% weight loss if overweight or obese.
Prioritize 7-9 hours of quality sleep per night.
Manage stress through techniques such as mindfulness, meditation, or yoga.
Avoid smoking and limit alcohol consumption.

followUp.frequency: Every 3-6 months initially, then every 6-12 months once stable and at goal, or more frequently if glycemic targets are not met or complications arise.
metrics: Fasting Plasma Glucose (FPG) and Postprandial Glucose (PPG)
Lipid Panel (annually or more frequently if not at goal)
Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate (eGFR) (annually or more frequently based on medication/CKD stage)

warnings: Metformin: Risk of lactic acidosis (rare but serious); contraindicated in severe renal impairment (eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m²).
SGLT2 Inhibitors: Risk of genitourinary infections, volume depletion, euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), and rare Fournier's gangrene.
GLP-1 Receptor Agonists: Risk of pancreatitis, thyroid C-cell tumors (in rodents), and gastrointestinal side effects. Contraindicated in personal/family history of MTC or MEN 2.
Insulin/Sulfonylureas: High risk of hypoglycemia; educate patients on symptoms and management.
Blood Pressure Medications (ACEi/ARBs): Monitor for hyperkalemia and acute kidney injury, especially with concomitant use of SGLT2i or diuretics.
Polypharmacy: Review all medications regularly to avoid drug-drug interactions and simplify regimens.
Hypoglycemia Awareness: Patients with long-standing diabetes or frequent hypoglycemia may lose warning symptoms (hypoglycemia unawareness); educate on proactive monitoring.
Sick Day Management: Educate patients on managing diabetes during illness, including medication adjustments and monitoring for dehydration/ketosis.
